description: An extensible, LLM-agnostic storytelling engine that allows users to build, extend, and retell interactive stories through structured folders, dynamic memory, character-driven logic, and local/API LLM integration. Supports rollback, NSFW-safe routing, world modeling, and plugin-based extensibility.
goals: Support modular storypack structure (characters, world, items, meta, style, config)
Enable multi-model integration using both local (Ollama) and remote (OpenAI, Groq, Claude) LLMs
Allow story state continuity across interactions with rollback and snapshot support
Support style-guided storytelling and configurable tone via per-story style guides
Enable immersive character memory and voice modeling, unique per character
Support token-efficient prompt construction and fallback routing between models
Expose OpenChronicle functions through CLI, Discord bot, and web UI
Support converter scripts to package/unpack storypacks and build from raw notes
Maintain LLM abstraction so any compliant model can be plugged in
Keep system compatible with low-resource hardware (NAS, Pi, etc.)
core_modules: scene_logger.py → logs scene_id, user/model input, memory state, flags, canon used
story_loader.py → loads storypacks (canon, memory, characters, meta.yaml, style)
context_builder.py → builds prompts using memory + canon + analysis + user input
content_analyzer.py → uses LLM to classify tone, flags, route models
memory_manager.py → stores/retrieves world state, character status, etc
database.py → story-local SQLite persistence with rollback and memory tracking
model_adapter.py → abstraction for different LLM providers (OpenAI, Ollama, mock)
rollback_engine.py → complete rollback system with backup and integrity validation
search_engine.py → full-text search with FTS5, caching, and advanced queries
bookmark_manager.py → comprehensive bookmark system with metadata and navigation
timeline_builder.py → timeline generation with scene and bookmark integration
character_style_manager.py → character consistency management across models
token_manager.py → intelligent token management and optimization
design_principles: Modular architecture with clean separation of concerns
Memory versioning and comprehensive rollback support
Scene tracking and timeline export capabilities

project_structure.storypacks: Per-story folders with canon, characters, memory, and style guides (JSON format)
project_structure.templates: Comprehensive JSON templates for creating new storypacks
project_structure.storage: SQLite databases per story, with exports and backups folders

Token management and optimization system
Flexible Docker deployment with optional embedded Ollama
Optional two-tier LLM architecture for token optimization
Smart context selection and token optimization
Core functionality test suite (56/56 tests passing)
Configuration management system with extensive provider support
Virtual environment setup with proper dependency management
Multiple deployment options (Docker, local, cloud)
Complete modularity - use any combination of features
Clean, stable codebase foundation
Centralized logging and utilities system
CLI commands: memory, models, switch, rollback (functional in interactive mode)
Complete templates system with JSON standardization
Original demo story 'Chronicle of Seasons' with rich world-building
Comprehensive Docker optimization with Alpine Linux and security hardening
Advanced backup and utilities system with comprehensive maintenance tools
Full Windows/Linux cross-platform compatibility with robust file handling

roadmap.title: ROUTING + CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM
details: Integrate lightweight classifier (e.g. DistilBERT or similar) to flag NSFW, emotional, or tonal shifts in user content
Route flagged content to appropriate model (e.g. uncensored Ollama model vs. OpenAI API)
Maintain tone continuity across model switches by injecting recent character memory and style guide fragments
Define routing behavior per-character and per-scene via config/storyname/models.json or routing_rules.json
Ensure logs contain traceable routing decisions for debug and user audit
Use model fallback chains with retry logic if a model fails, exceeds tokens, or encounters safety filter
Optionally run classifier in parallel with context builder to minimize latency
Support future override flags (e.g. /force-nsfw, /override-model) for power users or testing

architecture_highlights: LLM Support: 12+ providers with unified interface (see config/models.json)
Flexible deployment: Docker (with optional Ollama), local development, or cloud
Optional two-tier LLM system: Local LLM (analysis) + Main LLM (generation)
Each story gets its own SQLite database at storage/{story_id}/openchronicle.db
Tables: scenes, memory, memory_history, rollback_points, rollback_backups, bookmarks, scenes_fts, memory_fts
Static content (canon, characters) remains in YAML/text files
Dynamic data (scenes, memory, rollback, bookmarks, search indexes) stored in SQLite
Model adapters configured via config/models.json with extensive provider support
Optional content analysis with token optimization (50-80% reduction)
Centralized logging system with utilities for maintenance and monitoring
testing_status: Core system: 56/56 tests passing (100% success rate). Search engine: 11/101 tests failing (significant improvement from 41 failures). Windows compatibility issues resolved.
current_status: Production-ready core with comprehensive templates system and original demo content. Search engine needs Windows compatibility fixes. Ready for GitHub repository upload pending final validation.
